content:
JavaScript only developers are becoming a smaller and smaller category.

5 min read
2 hours ago

--

I know the title sounds annoying.
“JavaScript is dead” is the kind of thing people say when they want clicks, arguments, or both.
And technically, JavaScript is not dead. Obviously. It runs the web. TypeScript compiles to JavaScript. React apps still end up as JavaScript. Node still runs JavaScript. The browser still speaks JavaScript.
So no, JavaScript is not dead in the literal sense.
But JavaScript only development is dying.
That is the part people get weirdly defensive about.
Read this full article for free here!!!
TypeScript already won in a lot of places
The shift is not subtle anymore.
A few years ago, TypeScript felt optional. Some teams used it. Some people hated it. Some people treated it like extra homework added by backend developers who missed Java.
Now it is different.
A lot of modern front-end jobs expect TypeScript. Sometimes the job title says JavaScript developer, but the stack says TypeScript. Sometimes the interview says React, but the codebase is TypeScript. Sometimes nobody even mentions it because they assume you already know it.
